'''柳本大塚'''(やなぎもとおおつか）
*柳本古墳群に属する[[古墳]] == 概要 == * 所在地 ：[[天理市]]柳本町大塚 * 埋葬者 ：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期前半。 * 墳　形 ：[[前方後円墳]]。周濠は墳丘西において未確認。 * 規　模 ：全長約94m、後円径54m、 *埋葬施設：主室は墳丘中央部の南北主軸に沿う竪穴式石室、刳舟状木棺、鏡埋納石室を設ける。 *出土遺物： **大型内行花文鏡（鋼鏡・径39.7cm・宮内庁所蔵） **銅鏃×6 **鉄片 == 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]] {{History-stub}} [[Category:考古学|やなぎもとおおつか]] [[Category:日本の考古遺跡|やなぎもとおおつか]] [[Category:古墳|やなぎもとおおつか]] [[Category:古墳時代|やなぎもとおおつか]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史|やなぎもとおおつか]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=柳本大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] |所在地=奈良県天理市柳本町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長94m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室、刳舟状木棺 |築造年代=古墳時代前期初頭 |被葬者= |出土品=大型内行花文鏡 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}
'''柳本大塚古墳'''（やなぎもとおおつかこふん）とは、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[古墳]]である。[[柳本古墳群]]に属する。
== 概要 == * 所在地：[[天理市]]柳本町大塚 * 埋葬者：不明 * 築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期前半。 * 墳形：[[前方後円墳]]。後円部正円形ではない。前方部の正面は弧状であるが、撥形のように広がってはおらずむしろすぼまっている。周濠は墳丘西において未確認。 * 規模：全長約94m。後円径54m。 * 埋葬施設：主室は後円部墳丘中央部の南北主軸に沿う[[竪穴式石室]]（長さ約3.6メートル、幅約3.6メートル）、舟形の刳抜式木棺（長さ約2メートル、幅約1.2メートル）で同棺からは同鏃と鉄器が出土した。直径1メートルの不正円形の小石室が設けられ、径39.7センチの大型仿製内行花文鏡が納められていた。この小石室は、板石が天井の方にせり出した合掌式石室で副葬品埋納の副室と考えられている。 * 出土遺物 ** 大型内行花文鏡（鋼鏡・径39.7cm・宮内庁所蔵、四葉座間・連弧文間・雷雲文帯に配置された単位文様は渦巻き文のみ。四葉座間・連弧文間の周囲は珠文を地の文の様に隙間なく埋められている。） ** 銅鏃×6 ** 鉄片
== 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やなきもとおおつか こふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=柳本大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] |所在地=奈良県天理市柳本町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=全長94m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室、刳舟状木棺 |築造年代=古墳時代前期初頭 |被葬者= |出土品=大型内行花文鏡 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=柳本大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] |所在地=[[奈良県]][[天理市]][[柳本町]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長94m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室、刳舟状木棺 |築造年代=[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 |被葬者= |出土品=[[大型内行花文鏡]] |史跡指定= |特記事項= }}
'''柳本大塚古墳'''（やなぎもとおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[古墳]]である。[[柳本古墳群]]に属する。
== 概要 == * 所在地：[[天理市]][[柳本町]]大塚 * 埋葬者：不明 * 築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期前半。 * 墳形：[[前方後円墳]]。後円部正円形ではない。前方部の正面は弧状であるが、撥形のように広がってはおらずむしろすぼまっている。周濠は墳丘西において未確認。 * 規模：全長約94m。後円径54m。 * 埋葬施設：主室は後円部墳丘中央部の南北主軸に沿う[[竪穴式石室]]（長さ約3.6メートル、幅約3.6メートル）、舟形の刳抜式[[木棺]]（長さ約2メートル、幅約1.2メートル）で同棺からは同鏃と鉄器が出土した。直径1メートルの不正円形の小石室が設けられ、径39.7センチの大型仿製内行花文鏡が納められていた。この小石室は、板石が天井の方にせり出した合掌式石室で[[副葬品]]埋納の副室と考えられている。 * 出土遺物 ** 大型[[内行花文鏡]]（鋼鏡・径39.7cm・[[宮内庁]]所蔵、四葉座間・連弧文間・雷雲文帯に配置された単位文様は渦巻き文のみ。四葉座間・連弧文間の周囲は珠文を地の文の様に隙間なく埋められている。） ** 銅鏃×6 ** 鉄片 <gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|内行花文鏡 </gallery>
== 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | Location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
- **Location**: Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture - **Buried individual**: Unknown - **Construction period**: Early Kofun period - **Tumulus shape**: Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. - **Dimensions**: Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- **Large Naiko Kamon Mirror**: - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - **Bronze arrowheads**: Six pieces - **Iron fragments**: Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster]]
{{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Tomb|ja|柳本大塚古墳|draft}} is a [[Kofun]] in Japan It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Tumulus Group|ja|柳本古墳群|draft}} just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]] [[File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|thumb|A [[Flower mirror]] unearthed [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] from {{ill|Yanagimoto Otsuka Tomb|ja|柳本大塚古墳|draft}}, displayed in [[Tokyo National Museum]]]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
- **location**: Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture - **Buried individual**: Unknown - **Construction period**: Early Kofun period - **Tumulus shape**: Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. - **Dimensions**: Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- **Large Naiko Kamon Mirror**: - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - **Bronze arrowheads**: Six pieces - **Iron fragments**: Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': - Material: Bronze - Diameter: 39.7 cm - Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>
== Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces [[Category:dd]] Large Naiko Kamon Mirror<gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror </gallery>A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] [[Category:dd]] <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces [[Category:dd]] <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution.
