[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Tumulus shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. '''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
- '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. - '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces - '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery>== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}}
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''Construction period''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}}

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''Dimensions''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== Excavated Artifacts ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun shape''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|map_size:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | Construction_period = Early Kofun period | Buried_person = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. '''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. Artifacts such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
