{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}}
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|map_size:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|map_size:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. * '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}} [[ceb:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}}
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|map_size:20000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large naiko kamon mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}} [[ceb:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun]]

Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|ja|大型内行花文鏡|12=draft}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=柳本大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] |所在地=[[奈良県]][[天理市]][[柳本町]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|4|N|135|50|31|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長94m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室、刳舟状木棺 |築造年代=[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 |被葬者= |出土品=[[大型内行花文鏡]] |史跡指定= |特記事項= |地図=Japan Nara|アイコン=前方後円墳-南}}
'''柳本大塚古墳'''（やなぎもとおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]にある[[古墳]]である。[[柳本古墳群]]に属する。
== 概要 == * 所在地：[[天理市]][[柳本町]]大塚 * 埋葬者：不明 * 築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期前半。 * 墳形：[[前方後円墳]]。後円部正円形ではない。前方部の正面は弧状であるが、撥形のように広がってはおらずむしろすぼまっている。周濠は墳丘西において未確認。 * 規模：全長約94m。後円径54m。 * 埋葬施設：主室は後円部墳丘中央部の南北主軸に沿う[[竪穴式石室]]（長さ約3.6メートル、幅約3.6メートル）、舟形の刳抜式[[木棺]]（長さ約2メートル、幅約1.2メートル）で同棺からは同鏃と鉄器が出土した。直径1メートルの不正円形の小石室が設けられ、径39.7センチの大型仿製内行花文鏡が納められていた。この小石室は、板石が天井の方にせり出した合掌式石室で[[副葬品]]埋納の副室と考えられている。また鏡を単独で副葬していたことから、鏡を格別に重視していたと考えられる。 * 出土遺物 **[[内行花文鏡]]（鋼鏡・径39.7cm・[[宮内庁]]所蔵、四葉座間・連弧文間・雷雲文帯に配置された単位文様は渦巻き文のみ。四葉座間・連弧文間の周囲は珠文を地の文の様に隙間なく埋められている。） ** 銅鏃×6 ** 鉄片 <gallery widths="200px" heights="200px"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|大型内行花文鏡 </gallery>
== 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やなきもとおおつか こふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:柳本古墳群]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[List of kofun in Japan]]

The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[List of kofun in Japan]]

The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.

Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.551111|N|135.841944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large naiko kamon mirror]], bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}}

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[List of kofun in Japan]]

== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]
</gallery>

== Overview ==

The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.

Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
