{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.551111|N|135.841944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large naiko kamon mirror]], bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a [[:en:Large flower mirror|Large flower mirror]], measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}}

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|WD=Q11433747|lt=Large flower mirror}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}
</gallery>

'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]

== Overview ==

* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture
* '''Buried individual''': Unknown
* '''built''': Early Kofun period
* '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side.
* '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.  

The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.

A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|WD=Q11433747|lt=Large flower mirror}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.

== burial goods ==

* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''':  
** Material: Bronze  
** Diameter: 39.7 cm  
** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} collection.  
* '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces  
* '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror
<gallery widths="200" heights="200">
File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}
</gallery>
== See Also ==
* [[List of kofun in Japan]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.551111|N|135.841944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|WD=Q11433747|lt=Large naiko kamon mirror|1=Large flower mirror}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|WD=Q11433747|lt=Large flower mirror|1=Large flower mirror}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun (柳本大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Yanagimoto Ohtsuka kohun aerial.jpg|240px]] | location = Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.551111|N|135.841944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 94 meters; rear mound diameter: 54 meters | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, logboat-style wooden coffin | built = Early Kofun period | Interred_individual = Unknown | Excavated_items = {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|WD=Q11433747|lt=Large naiko kamon mirror|1=Large flower mirror}}, bronze arrowheads, iron fragments | Historic_site = None | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun''' (柳本大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, just north of the [[Makimuku ruins]]
== Overview ==
* '''location''': Yanagimoto Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried individual''': Unknown * '''built''': Early Kofun period * '''Kofun type''': Keyhole-shaped tumulus. The rear mound is not a perfect circle, and the front portion curves gently without flaring outward. The moat surrounding the mound is unconfirmed on the western side. * '''area''': Total length of approximately 94 meters; rear mound diameter of 54 meters.
The burial facility consists of a vertical stone chamber aligned along the north-south axis of the central rear mound. Within the chamber was a logboat-style hollowed wooden coffin measuring approximately 2 meters in length and 1.2 meters in width. burial goods such as bronze arrowheads and iron tools were discovered inside the coffin.
A secondary, irregularly shaped circular stone chamber with a diameter of 1 meter was also found. This chamber contained a {{ill|Large flower mirror|en|Large flower mirror|WD=Q11433747|lt=Large flower mirror|1=Large flower mirror}}, measuring 39.7 cm in diameter. The chamber featured a gabled roof structure with protruding ceiling stones and likely served as an auxiliary chamber for burial goods. The emphasis on the mirror, buried alone, suggests its special significance.
== burial goods ==
* '''Large Naiko Kamon Mirror''': ** Material: Bronze ** Diameter: 39.7 cm ** Design: Features quadrilobe and linked-arc motifs interspersed with thundercloud patterns. Surrounding these motifs are bead-shaped patterns densely arranged to fill the spaces. This mirror is currently held in the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} collection. * '''Bronze arrowheads''': Six pieces * '''Iron fragments''': Various small pieces <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09133.jpg|alt=Large Naiko Kamon Mirror|Large Naiko Kamon Mirror <gallery widths="200" heights="200"> File:Naikoukamon-kyo Tokyo National Museum.jpg|A [[flower mirror]] unearthed from the Kofun on display at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} </gallery> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} ==References== {{reflist|draft}}
